subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Related Rates & Riemann Sums — Melting Ice Cone
stimulus:
An ice sculpture melts in such a way that it can be modeled as a cone that maintains a conical shape as it decreases in size. The radius of the base of the cone is given by a twice-differentiable function $r$, where $r(t)$ is measured in centimeters and $t$ is measured in days. The table below gives selected values of $r'(t)$, the rate of change of the radius, over the time interval $0 \leq t \leq 12$.

| $t$ (days) | 0 | 3 | 7 | 10 | 12 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $r'(t)$ (cm/day) | $-6.1$ | $-5.0$ | $-4.4$ | $-3.8$ | $-3.5$ |

parts.prompt: Approximate $r''(8.5)$ using the average rate of change of $r'$ over the interval $7 \leq t \leq 10$. Show the computations that lead to your answer, and indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: Correct units: centimeters per day per day, written as $\text{cm/day}^2$.

required_elements: Units stated as centimeters per day per day (or $\text{cm/day}^2$)

common_traps: Omitting units or writing cm/day instead of cm/day$^2$

parts.prompt: Is there a time $t$, $0 \leq t \leq 3$, for which $r'(t) = -5.5$? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: States that $r'(0) = -6.1$ and $r'(3) = -5.0$, and observes that $-6.1 < -5.5 < -5.0$, so $-5.5$ is strictly between $r'(0)$ and $r'(3)$.

Concludes **yes** and invokes the Intermediate Value Theorem: since $r'$ is continuous on $[0, 3]$ (because $r$ is twice-differentiable) and $-5.5$ is between $r'(0) = -6.1$ and $r'(3) = -5.0$, the IVT guarantees that there exists a $t \in (0, 3)$ such that $r'(t) = -5.5$.

required_elements: Yes, by the Intermediate Value Theorem, since $r'$ is continuous and $-5.5$ is between $r'(0)$ and $r'(3)$

parts.prompt: Use a right Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated in the table to approximate the value of $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{12} r'(t)\,dt$.

scoring_points.description:
Sets up the right Riemann sum using the four subintervals $[0,3]$, $[3,7]$, $[7,10]$, $[10,12]$ with right endpoints $t = 3, 7, 10, 12$:
$$\int_0^{12} r'(t)\,dt \approx (3)\cdot r'(3) + (4)\cdot r'(7) + (3)\cdot r'(10) + (2)\cdot r'(12)$$
$$= 3(-5.0) + 4(-4.4) + 3(-3.8) + 2(-3.5)$$
common_traps: Using left endpoints instead of right endpoints
Using equal subinterval widths instead of the actual widths from the table

parts.prompt: The height of the cone decreases at a rate of $2$ centimeters per day. At time $t = 3$ days, the radius is $100$ centimeters and the height is $50$ centimeters. Find the rate of change of the volume of the cone with respect to time, in cubic centimeters per day, at time $t = 3$ days. (The volume $V$ of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.)

scoring_points.description:
Correctly differentiates $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$ with respect to $t$ using the product rule:
$$\frac{dV}{dt} = \frac{\pi}{3}\left(2r\frac{dr}{dt}\cdot h + r^2\cdot\frac{dh}{dt}\right)$$
Substitutes the given values at $t = 3$: $r = 100$, $h = 50$, $\dfrac{dr}{dt} = r'(3) = -5.0$, and $\dfrac{dh}{dt} = -2$.

Correct answer:
$$\frac{dV}{dt} = \frac{\pi}{3}\left(2(100)(50)(-5) + (100)^2(-2)\right) = \frac{\pi}{3}\left(-50000 - 20000\right) = -\frac{70000\pi}{3}$$
cubic centimeters per day.
